metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the model hit the token cap before finishing (finish_reason=length) — its output is TRUNCATED; raise max_tokens or split the step

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact function/module constituting 'reconciler startup' where the committed-file cleanup helper must be wired (e.g., `ReconcilerEngine.__init__`, `reconcile_once`, or the CLI `run` command), so the invocation site is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The 'One-time cleanup' bullet instructs the plan author to 'state the site explicitly' for the reconciler startup invocation of the committed-file migration helper, but the plan never actually states that site. An executing agent cannot know which function, module, or hook is meant by 'reconciler startup.'
findings.location: What > One-time cleanup bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'invoked from exactly one site — reconciler startup; state the site explicitly' with the actual qualified name, e.g. 'invoked from `src/rebar/reconciler/_engine.py:ReconcilerEngine.startup()` before the first lock acquisition'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion that exercises the ref lock end-to-end in a non-mocked scenario after the file backend is removed — e.g., 'a reconciler integration test completes a full lock/unlock cycle using the ref backend against a real (ephemeral) git repo and passes CI.'
findings.finding: After the file backend is deleted, the `ref` lock becomes the sole code path for every lock acquisition, but no AC in this plan exercises that path end-to-end (e.g. a real reconciler cycle against a real git repo). The only verification is 'full non-integration suite green,' which by definition excludes integration scenarios and may rely on mocks that bypass the ref lock entirely.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria > AC1 and AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC3 a requirement for at least one unit/integration test covering: (a) idempotent invocation (calling the migration twice produces the same result), and (b) git failure causes a log entry but does not raise/abort.
findings.finding: AC3 specifies behavioral requirements for the `.gitattributes` update-migration (idempotent, log-and-continue on git failure) but does not require any test to verify those behaviors. Without an explicit test requirement, the migration could be shipped untested and 'full suite green' would still pass.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria > AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion covering the ref-lock ADR update (e.g., 'The ref-lock ADR records that the b859 retry loop is superseded by the C3 self-healing lock').
findings.finding: The body describes a concrete deliverable — 'Extend the ref-lock ADR to record that the b859 loop is superseded by the C3 self-healing lock' — but no acceptance criterion covers it, leaving it unverifiable and potentially skipped during review.
findings.location: What section, last bullet; Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Add as AC6: '[ ] The ref-lock ADR is updated to document that the `b859` retry loop is retired and replaced by the C3 ref-lock self-healing mechanism.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the `file` backend removal (e.g., how to revert if the ref backend regresses) and acknowledge/handle the case where `lock_backend = file` is set explicitly in existing configs (validation error vs. silent coercion to `ref`).
findings.finding: The plan removes the `file` lock backend entirely but does not acknowledge or provide a rollback/back-out path for deployments that have `lock_backend = file` set explicitly in their config — those instances will fail at startup with no migration or graceful fallback.
findings.location: What section ('Flip `[reconciler] lock_backend` default to `ref` and remove the `file` backend path'); AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly whether the config schema accepts unknown keys (silent drop vs. error) and, if strict, add a migration note instructing operators to remove `lock_max_retries` from their config; or add a coercion shim that logs a deprecation warning and ignores the key on first boot after upgrade.
findings.finding: The plan retires the `lock_max_retries` config key by removing its schema entry and coercion logic, but does not state whether existing configs that still contain this key will be silently ignored or will cause a validation/startup error — an unacknowledged compatibility break for any deployed config that references this key.
findings.location: What section ('Retire the config key `lock_max_retries` (`_config_schema.py:334`, coercion `:445`)'); AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a changelog entry / operator migration note documenting the removal of `lock_max_retries` and `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES`, and specify whether unknown keys are warned or fatal post-removal.
findings.finding: Removal of the `lock_max_retries` config key and its env aliases (`REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES` + deprecated alias) is a user-facing breaking change with no mention of a changelog entry, deprecation notice, or user-facing migration guide.
findings.location: What section / AC line 1: '…the `lock_max_retries` config key (`_config_schema.py:334`, coercion `:445`) + env aliases (`REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES` + deprecated alias)…'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a CHANGELOG.md / migration-guide entry: 'The `lock_max_retries` config key and `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES` env var are removed; the ref-lock backend (C3) provides self-healing and no longer requires a retry budget. Remove these settings from your config files and environment.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific function/module (e.g., `reconciler._startup_migrations()` or `init.py:_run_startup_migrations`) where the committed-lock cleanup migration will be registered, so the coupling point is explicit in the plan and in code.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the committed-lock cleanup migration must be 'invoked from exactly one site — reconciler startup; state the site explicitly,' but the plan itself never names the specific function, module, or call-site where this invocation will live.
findings.location: What section: 'One-time cleanup: remove any pre-existing `.reconciler-pass-lock`/`.reconciler-phase-gate` committed on the tickets branch… invoked from exactly one site — reconciler startup; state the site explicitly'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the config loader handles `lock_max_retries` in existing config files after the key is removed from the schema (e.g., warn-and-ignore with a deprecation log, or document that an unknown-key error is expected and users must scrub their configs).
findings.finding: The plan removes `lock_max_retries` from the config schema (`_config_schema.py:334,445`) and its env aliases, but does not specify what happens when an existing config file still contains `lock_max_retries = <value>` after the key is retired — whether the config loader will error, warn-and-ignore, or silently drop the unknown key.
findings.location: AC line 1 / _config_schema.py:334,445
findings.suggested_fix: Add an 'unknown key' tolerance pass (or confirm the loader already has one) and emit a deprecation warning log when `lock_max_retries` is encountered, so operators know to remove it rather than hitting a cryptic parse error.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the behavior when `lock_backend = file` is present in existing config after the file backend is removed: either reject with a clear, actionable error message or auto-migrate to `ref` with a logged warning.
findings.finding: The plan removes the `file` backend implementation from `_advisory_lock.py` but does not specify what happens when an existing repo's config file explicitly sets `lock_backend = file` after the backend is removed — whether this causes a hard error at startup or a graceful fallback.
findings.location: What section / _advisory_lock.py (file backend removal)
findings.suggested_fix: At startup, if `lock_backend` is set to a no-longer-supported value, log a clear warning ('file backend has been retired; falling back to ref') and proceed with `ref`, or raise a ConfigurationError with an explicit migration instruction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit error handling (log-and-continue, matching the gitattributes migration) to the `.reconciler-*` committed-file removal migration helper, and specify safe partial-state behavior if the git operation fails mid-flight.
findings.finding: The plan specifies error handling (log-and-continue) for the `.gitattributes` update-migration git failure, but is silent on error handling for the one-time migration that removes pre-existing committed `.reconciler-pass-lock`/`.reconciler-phase-gate` files — no failure mode (git rm failure, commit failure, dirty-tree conflict) is addressed.
findings.location: What section — 'One-time cleanup' / AC line 4
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the gitattributes migration pattern: wrap the git rm + commit in a try/except, log the failure with context (e.g., git stderr), and continue startup without aborting — document that a subsequent startup will retry idempotently.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact file path and function (e.g. `reconciler/startup.py::_run_startup_migrations`) where the one-time `.reconciler-*` cleanup helper is invoked, and add that reference to AC4 so the criterion is end-state-observable without reading code.
findings.finding: The plan body says the committed-files cleanup migration should be '(invoked from exactly one site — reconciler startup; state the site explicitly)' but neither the body nor AC4 names the actual file path or function where the startup invocation will live. Without naming the site, the criterion lacks an end-state-observable proof and the implementer has no unambiguous contract.
findings.location: What section — 'One-time cleanup' bullet; AC item 4
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the 'One-time cleanup' bullet: 'Called from `<module>::<function>` (the single reconciler startup entry point) immediately after the `.gitattributes` migration.' Update AC4 to match.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a live/end-to-end acceptance criterion (e.g., a non-mocked integration smoke test that exercises the `ref` lock backend with `lock_backend = ref` as the default config) so this ticket's own suite cannot go green while the newly-defaulted path is broken.
findings.finding: The plan makes `lock_backend = ref` the new default (a cutover) and deletes the file backend, but adds no live/end-to-end acceptance criterion in this ticket for the newly-defaulted `ref` path. All test work in this ticket is deletions of old tests and updates to gitattributes/config assertions. The plan defers ref-backend validation to dependency ticket C3 (2711-21bf-ab71-4136), which is a prerequisite, not a named child ticket — so the proxy-validation exemption for 'explicitly deferred to a NAMED child ticket' does not apply. This ticket's suite can go green (old tests deleted, new tests all passing) while the live `ref`-backend path is broken under the new default.
findings.location: AC #1 / What section: 'Flip lock_backend defaults to ref' / _advisory_lock.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC #1: 'An integration/smoke test (or extension of an existing integration suite) exercises the reconciler startup with `lock_backend` at its new default value (`ref`) against a real git repo, asserting the lock is acquired and released without error.' This does not need to be a full end-to-end test — a single non-mocked lock acquire/release cycle with the real ref backend suffices.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] After removing the `file` backend path, also remove the `lock_backend` config key from `_config_schema.py` (ReconcilerConfig dataclass and its coercion entry) and its env alias, OR add an explicit validation that errors on `lock_backend = file` with a clear deprecation message — do not leave a dead toggle in the config schema.
findings.finding: After C4 removes the file-lock backend, the `lock_backend` config key (introduced in C3) has only one valid value (`ref`). The plan flips its default but does not remove it, leaving dead config surface — a user or operator setting `lock_backend = file` would silently reference a deleted code path.
findings.location: What section: 'Flip `[reconciler] lock_backend` default to `ref` and remove the `file` backend path'; AC item 1
findings.suggested_fix: In `_config_schema.py`, remove the `lock_backend` field from `ReconcilerConfig` and its coercion lambda from the reconciler section. Drop the corresponding env alias. If a hard removal is too abrupt, add a `_as_choice(v, k, {"ref"})` validator with a clear error message directing users to remove the stale setting.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the deletion scope in the plan to explicitly include the file-backend CAS internals (`_jittered_backoff`, `_cas_backoff_seconds`, `_cas_advance_with_retry`, `_commit_in_detached_tickets_worktree`, `_write_file_to_tickets_branch`, `_delete_file_from_tickets_branch`, `_CAS_RETRY_BUDGET`/`_CAS_BACKOFF_*` constants) and add `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_advisory_lock_cas_race.py` to the list of test files to delete/adjust alongside `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py`.
findings.finding: The plan's deletion scope for `_advisory_lock.py` is incomplete: it names the b859 outer-retry helpers for deletion but omits the file-backend CAS internals that also become dead code when the file backend is removed. Specifically `_jittered_backoff` (line 134), `_cas_backoff_seconds` (line 429), `_cas_advance_with_retry` (line 447), `_commit_in_detached_tickets_worktree` (line 490), `_write_file_to_tickets_branch` (line 588), `_delete_file_from_tickets_branch` (line 618), and their constants (`_CAS_RETRY_BUDGET`, `_CAS_BACKOFF_*`) all only serve the file-write path. Correspondingly, `test_advisory_lock_cas_race.py` is not listed for deletion/adjustment alongside `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py`.
findings.location: What section: 'Remove the b859-8fa1 retry loop … AND its now-dead helpers'; AC item 1; `_advisory_lock.py` lines 108-111, 134-158, 429-444, 447-585, 588-658
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC item 1 and the 'Remove the b859-8fa1 retry loop' bullet, append: 'also delete `_jittered_backoff` (:134–158), `_cas_backoff_seconds` (:429–444), `_cas_advance_with_retry` (:447–488), `_commit_in_detached_tickets_worktree` (:490–585), `_write_file_to_tickets_branch` (:588–616), `_delete_file_from_tickets_branch` (:618–658), and the constants `_CAS_RETRY_BUDGET` / `_CAS_BACKOFF_*` (:108–111); delete `test_advisory_lock_cas_race.py`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that ticket C3 (2711-21bf-ab71-4136) is merged and `lock_backend` is present in `_config_schema.py` before this plan's AC1 (flip default, delete file backend) is actionable; add a precondition check or gate in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `lock_backend` already exists as a config key to be 'flipped' to `ref`, but no `lock_backend` field exists anywhere in `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` or `src/rebar/` — it is a net-new key introduced by the still-open upstream ticket C3 (2711-21bf-ab71-4136). The plan's core deletion premise (remove the `file` backend path) is unverifiable until C3 is merged.
findings.location: What section — 'Flip `[reconciler] lock_backend` default to `ref` and remove the `file` backend path from `_advisory_lock.py`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit precondition: 'Requires C3 (2711-21bf-ab71-4136) merged; verify `lock_backend` exists in `ReconcilerConfig` before proceeding.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py` must be **partially** adjusted (remove only the b859 outer-retry tests), not wholesale deleted; the CAS-helper tests (`test_cas_wrapper_binds_cas_constants`, `test_both_wrappers_apply_their_jitter_band_in_one_uniform_draw`, `test_jittered_backoff_formula_capped_no_jitter`) must be retained or migrated.
findings.finding: `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py` also contains tests for `_cas_backoff_seconds`, `_jittered_backoff`, and the `_CAS_*` constants (lines 231-278) — CAS-layer helpers that are NOT being retired by this plan (they serve the ref-lock CAS path). The plan directs 'Delete/adjust the tests that exercise them' without distinguishing these retained CAS tests, risking silent deletion of non-retired test coverage.
findings.location: What section — 'Delete/adjust the tests that exercise them: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Delete/adjust the tests' with 'Strip the b859 outer-retry tests (test_acquire_retries_on_drift, test_custom_budget, test_backoff_timing, test_budget_one, test_non_drift_fails_fast) from test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py; keep the CAS/jitter tests (lines 231-278).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_config_loader.py` and `tests/unit/test_config_typed.py` to the list of test files requiring adjustment when `lock_max_retries` is removed from `ReconcilerConfig`.
findings.finding: `test_config_loader.py` (line 135) and `test_config_typed.py` (lines 25, 37, 46) both reference `lock_max_retries` directly but are not listed in the plan's deletion/adjustment inventory for the `lock_max_retries` config key removal.
findings.location: What section — 'Delete/adjust the tests that exercise them: ... the retry-budget assertions in `tests/.../test_config_file_wiring.py` + `test_ev3c_env_renames.py`'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the 'Delete/adjust the tests' bullet to include: `tests/unit/test_config_loader.py` (line 135) and `tests/unit/test_config_typed.py` (lines 25, 37, 46).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py` must be split/pruned, not wholesale deleted: the b859-loop tests (budget/retry/sleep sections) are retired but the CAS-backoff tests (`test_jittered_backoff_formula_capped_no_jitter`, `test_cas_wrapper_binds_cas_constants`, `test_both_wrappers_apply_their_jitter_band_in_one_uniform_draw`) must be preserved or migrated to `test_advisory_lock_cas_race.py`.
findings.finding: `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py` also contains tests for `_cas_backoff_seconds`, `_jittered_backoff`, and `_cas_backoff_seconds`+`_CAS_*` constants (lines 231-278) that exercise CAS-path symbols the plan retains — deleting the whole file destroys surviving coverage of non-b859 logic.
findings.location: What section: 'Delete/adjust the tests that exercise them: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'Delete/adjust … test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py' to: 'Remove the b859-loop test cases (tests 1-5 covering `acquire_pass_lock` retry loop) from `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py`; migrate or keep the four CAS-backoff tests (lines 231-278) that cover `_jittered_backoff` / `_cas_backoff_seconds`, which are retained.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_config_typed.py`, `tests/unit/test_config_loader.py`, and `src/rebar/config.py` (the `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET` alias entry at lines 448-452) to the enumerated edit targets for the `lock_max_retries` removal.
findings.finding: Three files that directly consume `lock_max_retries` are not enumerated as edit targets: `tests/unit/test_config_typed.py` (lines 25, 37, 46 assert `c.reconciler.lock_max_retries`), `tests/unit/test_config_loader.py` (line 135 asserts `lock_max_retries == 9`), and `src/rebar/config.py` (lines 448-452, the `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET` env-alias dispatch entry).
findings.location: What section: 'Retire the config key lock_max_retries (_config_schema.py:334, coercion :445) and its env aliases'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/config.md` to the edit targets: remove the `lock_max_retries` line (`:180`) and the `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET→REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES` entry (`:318`) from the aliases table.
findings.finding: `docs/config.md` documents `lock_max_retries` (line 180) and its env alias `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET` (line 318) but is not named as an edit target anywhere in the plan or ACs.
findings.location: What section: env aliases removal; AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state that `_jittered_backoff` and `_cas_backoff_seconds` are RETAINED (they serve the separate CAS-race retry path, not the b859 band-aid), and that only `_compute_backoff_seconds` + the b859-specific constants (`_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET_DEFAULT`, `_BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS`, `_BACKOFF_FACTOR`, `_BACKOFF_CAP_SECONDS`, `_BACKOFF_JITTER_FRACTION`) are deleted; update the test-deletion scope in AC-1 to match (the `test_jittered_backoff_formula_*` and `test_cas_wrapper_*` tests in `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py` cover retained code and must NOT be deleted).
findings.finding: _compute_backoff_seconds is listed as a 'now-dead helper' to delete, but _jittered_backoff — which _compute_backoff_seconds merely wraps — is also the shared implementation used by _cas_backoff_seconds and _cas_advance_with_retry (the retained CAS-race retry path, unrelated to b859). The plan's deletion boundary is ambiguous: an implementer cleaning up 'now-dead' code naturally deletes _jittered_backoff too, which silently breaks the CAS retry backoff. The plan gives no positive rationale for where the deletion boundary sits.
findings.location: What section — 'Remove the b859-8fa1 retry loop' bullet; _advisory_lock.py:134 (_jittered_backoff), :150 (_compute_backoff_seconds), :429 (_cas_backoff_seconds); AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Remove the b859-8fa1 retry loop' bullet, add: 'Retain `_jittered_backoff` (`:134`) and `_cas_backoff_seconds` (`:429`) — they serve `_cas_advance_with_retry`, not the b859 band-aid. Delete only `_compute_backoff_seconds` and the five b859 constants. In `test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py`, delete only the b859 tests (tests 1-5 / `test_default_budget_*`, `test_custom_budget_*`, `test_backoff_timing_*`, `test_budget_one_*`, `test_non_drift_*`); retain the `test_jittered_backoff_*` and `test_cas_wrapper_*` tests at the bottom of the file since they cover retained code.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state the call site for the committed-files cleanup migration: it must be invoked in `main()` BEFORE `check_pass_lock` (Step 2a), so stale `.reconciler-*` tree files cannot cause a false 'lock held' abort on the first post-migration run.
findings.finding: The plan says the one-time committed-files cleanup migration is 'invoked from exactly one site — reconciler startup; state the site explicitly', but the plan itself never states that site. The call point in `__main__.py` (which function, before/after the lock acquire, Step 2 or before Step 2a) is unspecified, which is a mechanism gap: running the migration AFTER acquiring the lock is different from running it BEFORE, and running it under the file backend vs. ref backend path matters for whether the now-absent files would interfere with `check_pass_lock`.
findings.location: What section — 'One-time cleanup' bullet; AC line 4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'One-time cleanup' bullet: 'Call the helper from `main()` in `__main__.py`, immediately before Step 2a (`check_pass_lock`), so that any stale committed `.reconciler-*` file is removed before the lock check runs. Do NOT place it inside `run_pass()` (that path is skipped by reconcile-check mode).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the AC1 directive to **delete** the file-lock backend code with a directive to **disable** it (leave the code path intact behind the `lock_backend=file` flag) until at least one full production reconciler cycle has completed successfully under `lock_backend=ref`; establish an explicit 'baked' criterion (e.g., N consecutive CI passes, or a named follow-up ticket) before the file backend code is removed.
findings.finding: The plan's core precondition — 'the ref-lock (C3) is proven' and 'ref backend must be wired and validated first' — is an unverified capability assertion: `_ref_lock` does not exist anywhere in the codebase, `lock_backend` config key does not exist, and dependent ticket C3 (2711-21bf-ab71-4136) is still `open`. By permanently deleting the file-lock backend path (not just flipping the default), the plan removes the only rollback mechanism for a novel architecture (blob-pointing custom `refs/reconciler/*` pushed to GitHub remote) whose GitHub compatibility was flagged as unverified in prior plan reviews on upstream tickets C1/C2/C3. The plan should not delete the file-backend code path until C3's capability has been demonstrated in production; the expand-contract switch (keep file backend, flip default) is the safer prior art for this class of migration.
findings.location: What section — 'Flip `lock_backend` default to `ref` and remove the `file` backend path from `_advisory_lock.py`'; AC line 1 — 'the file-lock backend … are deleted'
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC1 from 'the file-lock backend … are deleted' to 'the file-lock backend is disabled by default (lock_backend defaults to ref) but retained in code under lock_backend=file for one release cycle; a separate follow-up ticket schedules its deletion after production validation.' This is the standard expand-contract migration pattern used by the project for comparable changes (e.g., gc.auto migration retained --unset before removing gc.auto=0 entirely).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether a config-level rollback lever (e.g. `lock_backend=file`) is intentionally removed or whether a deprecation window with the file-backend code retained-but-warned should precede full deletion.
findings.finding: The file-lock backend code is fully deleted with no preserved rollback lever. C3 (the dependency) kept `lock_backend=file` as a revert switch; C4 removes that switch and the file backend code entirely. If the ref backend proves broken in a specific operator environment post-deploy, reverting requires a full code rollback — there is no config-level escape hatch.
findings.location: What section / AC line 1: 'remove the dead file-lock machinery'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the lock-file removal startup migration to explicitly state its partial-failure policy (log-and-continue vs. abort) and confirm idempotency under CAS-rejection, mirroring the `.gitattributes` arm's stated contract.
findings.finding: The failure-handling contract for the startup lock-file removal migration (removing committed `.reconciler-pass-lock`/`.reconciler-phase-gate`) is unspecified. The plan states 'log-and-continue on git failure' only for the `.gitattributes` migration; the lock-file cleanup helper has no stated partial-failure policy.
findings.location: What section: 'One-time cleanup: remove any pre-existing .reconciler-pass-lock/.reconciler-phase-gate' / AC line 4
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'on git/CAS failure, log a warning and continue — the migration will re-attempt on next startup' to the lock-file cleanup helper spec, matching the `.gitattributes` arm wording.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before this plan is executed, confirm that C1's AC0 feasibility spike result (refs/reconciler/* ref→blob push+fetch via GitHub CI token) is documented in the ref-lock ADR under `docs/adr/`; the plan must not proceed until that evidence artifact exists.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the ref-lock (C3) is proven' and directs flipping `lock_backend` default to `ref` and deleting the file-lock path, but `_ref_lock.py` does not exist in the codebase, no ADR for the ref-lock design exists under `docs/adr/`, and C1's AC0 feasibility spike ('prove `refs/reconciler/*` ref→blob round-trips through the GitHub remote with the CI token') is not marked complete — leaving the core external capability (non-branch ref push/fetch over GitHub) unverified at plan-writing time.
findings.location: What section — 'Flip `[reconciler] lock_backend` default to `ref`'; AC1 — 'the file-lock backend … are deleted'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a concrete precondition check to the plan's 'What' section: 'Verify the ref-lock ADR exists at docs/adr/ with a recorded AC0 spike result before beginning any deletions; if the ADR is absent, this plan must not proceed.' This converts the implicit dependency on C3-being-proven into a machine-checkable gate.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
